Document:
# Blueprints & Crafting
Blueprint crafting is the workbench-based side of the Wasteland crafting system. If you have the right blueprint, bench, resources, and tools, you can turn scrap and rare parts into faction gear, armor, and weapons.
## Crafting Basics
Blueprints define what you can make and what each recipe needs.
Workbenches determine where a recipe can be completed.
Resources can be raw materials, scrap parts, or intermediate crafted components.
Tools are required by many recipes, but they are not consumed during crafting.
Some blueprints are faction-locked, so access can depend on who you are and which bench you can use.
## How to Craft
Find or buy a blueprint, gather the listed resources, then take everything to the correct workbench. Open the crafting interface, select the blueprint, and confirm the recipe.
If the recipe is available and you meet the requirements, the crafted item will appear in your inventory or next to the bench.
## Blueprint Categories
Blueprints are found by exploring the Wasteland, looting enemies, or trading with faction vendors. Click an entry to see its full recipe and workbench requirements.
### Brotherhood of Steel
Power armor restoration and Brotherhood field gear, usually recovered from patrols, bunkers, and chapter stockpiles.
### NCR
NCR armor patterns and restored military equipment, most commonly found in camps, depots, and quartermaster caches.
### Legion
Legion armor blueprints recovered from outposts, patrol leaders, and veteran warriors.
### Tribal
Handcrafted tribal weapons and armor, usually traded by tribal groups or found in remote camps.
### Miscellaneous
Faction-neutral blueprints available to any capable crafter with the right bench and parts.
